newsSinceJun6: No discrete material news since June 6. Today's -6.1% to $64.50 looks flow-driven: digestion of the run into the June 4 inaugural industry-analyst briefing plus a Summit Insights downgrade to Hold citing H2-2026 supply-chain risk. The briefing (June 4, just before the window) disclosed 15+ robotics design wins with >$100M lifetime revenue and a Hanwha long-term agreement with >$800M potential revenue over 10+ years. The GoPro/Insta360 ITC overhang was resolved in Insta360's favor in March 2026 and is not a live risk.

sources.label: StockTitan - Ambarella inaugural quarterly edge AI analyst briefing
Yahoo Finance - Ambarella's largest customer avoids US import restrictions in ITC final ruling (Mar 11, 2026)
Yahoo Finance - Did Ambarella's new edge AI analyst briefings reframe its go-to-market strategy?
Kavout - What just happened to Ambarella stock (earlier ITC plunge context)

verdicts.rankRationale: Yesterday's removal case stands: AMBA has no direct CPO/HBM/advanced-packaging/test bottleneck exposure, and nothing since June 6 changes that. Today's -6.1% is flow-driven and cheapness does not create compute-node centrality, so no new move beyond the pending removal.
verdicts.bull: Edge AI is 80% of revenue growing ~50% YoY, and robotics design wins plus the Hanwha LTA show real physical-AI traction that could eventually justify a different node classification.
verdicts.neutral: A quality edge-AI SoC franchise, but on a compute-bottleneck node map it is peripheral; the score drift reflects that misfit rather than business deterioration.
verdicts.bear: Summit Insights flags H2-2026 supply-chain risk and the name remains loss-making on GAAP; it occupies a slot a cleaner bottleneck name should hold.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Edge-infrastructure or inference-node design win that gives AMBA direct AI-compute-chain relevance.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Continued misclassification drag: no CPO/HBM/packaging linkage means the node thesis never inflects.

verdicts.rankRationale: Yesterday's verdict was remove for weak CPO relevance; no new optical/co-packaged-optics evidence has emerged since June 6, so we hold rather than double-count. The sell-off improves entry price but not CPO-chain relevance, which is what this page ranks.
verdicts.bull: At $2.8B cap and -14% YTD, the name is genuinely under-owned, and the Cooper developer ecosystem plus 46M cumulative edge-AI SoC shipments give optionality if edge inference becomes a recognized bottleneck.
verdicts.neutral: Watchlist status remains correct: a useful ecosystem node with mixed under-discovery evidence and no direct CPO linkage.
verdicts.bear: Zero co-packaged-optics exposure means the name dilutes the page; single-customer concentration (Arashi Vision/Insta360) remains a structural fragility even after the favorable ITC ruling.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Any edge-AI infrastructure partnership that ties AMBA into the optical/interconnect supply chain.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Customer concentration plus H2-2026 supply-chain risk flagged by Summit Insights, with no CPO thesis to offset.

verdicts.rankRationale: This is the page where the thesis actually lives: the June 4 briefing quantified 15+ robotics design wins with >$100M lifetime revenue and a >$800M Hanwha LTA, and today's -6.1% flow-driven drop with that thesis intact improves asymmetry at $2.8B cap. Sell-off without fundamental robotics news is exactly the setup this alpha list rewards.
verdicts.bull: Quantified robotics design-win pipeline, 50% YoY edge-AI growth, and a decade-scale Hanwha agreement validate the local-perception-under-power-constraints thesis while the stock sits -14% YTD.
verdicts.neutral: Design wins are early-lifecycle and the briefing was a company-curated disclosure; rank 8 already reflects meaningful credit, so the move up should be modest.
verdicts.bear: Summit Insights' H2-2026 supply-chain warning could delay robotics SoC shipments, and AMBA is more discovered than deep-bench robotics sleepers, capping residual alpha.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Conversion of the 15+ robotics design wins into reported revenue ramps, or a named humanoid/AMR platform win.
verdicts.primaryRisk: H2-2026 supply-chain disruption or design-win slippage that pushes the robotics revenue ramp past FY2028.
